The grand finale performance culminates with a surprise reveal of some more musical friends for the groups to meet and carry on singing with. But not before a rousing Christmas sing-song, tinsel and all(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Halifax looking around a terraced house, as well as casting his eye over a Wolverhampton semi. Martin Roberts visits Ashford, Kent to see a former pharmacy(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E and the team members need to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland. Meanwhile, occupational therapist Marc Beswick returns to long-term patient Summer. The four-year-old has a condition that means it's hard for her to move her arms and legs. Marc has been working on her electric wheelchair, making modifications to make sure she can develop some independence(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a hammock, a two-in-one colander and dish and a microfibre glove towel. Presented by Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for Christmas bargains at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while presenter Charlie Ross heads off to nearby Goldsborough Hall to learn about a special Royal Christmas gift sent to troops in the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi realises he is facing a long stint in prison when his bail is revoked. Daniel gets to the bottom of Bear's ambilivance towards Jimmi(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple in West Sussex to find their first shared home together on a budget of £700,000, and meets a craftsman keeping the art of trug making alive. The presenter also finds time to meet master craftsmen keeping the county's traditional trug-making heritage alive(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Andy and Danielle have moved into a new home in Teignmouth, Devon, and are desperate for a garden that reminds them of their wonderful holidays in Morocco. Luckily for them, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich hope to make their dream a reality and design a Marrakesh rhiad-inspired back garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Caner Rachael South restores a wicker cradle that has been in use for generations. With a new addition to the family on the way the owner hopes to be able to pass this on to her grandchild and keep the heirloom alive. Meanwhile, Brenton West fixes a much-loved powder compact, made in the shape of a piano. Toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch add the finishing touch with a new, hand-sewn pink powder pouf and a wall clock is also rejuvenated(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England midfielder Jill Scott, Olympic Taekwondo silver medallist Lutalo Muhammad, rugby league great Kevin Sinfield and Great Britain 400m runner Emily Diamond join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat tries to charm Kheerat in the hope of keeping her home - and while he agrees, he increases their rent at the same time, which does not go down well with Mo and Jean. Sharon and Louise assume that Phil's bad mood is down to baby Peggy still being in hospital. Jay leaves Stuart in charge once more only to regret when he and Rainie cover the place in Christmas decorations(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Charlie Brooker assumes the role of host, with journalist and broadcaster Emma Barnett and comedian Phil Wang joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Taking a place on Graham's festive sofa, from the new blockbuster Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker are John Boyega and Daisy Ridley, alongside Superman himself, Henry Cavill, who is appearing in new Netflix fantasy drama series The Witcher. Ruth Jones and Rob Brydon, reunited for the much-anticipated Gavin & Stacey Christmas special, also appear. Plus, Robbie Williams performs a song from his new album The Christmas Present, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The Priest takes Fleabag for some quiet reflection, leading to an unexpected revelation. After a day spent looking back on painful memories, she turns to him for solace, only to find more trouble. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, with Andrew Scott(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The two finalists take on their last challenge to win an investment from Lord Sugar, with contestants from the series returning to aid them in their endeavours. In an intensive three-day challenge, the finalists must create a new brand for their company, produce a digital billboard and make a television advert, before pitching to Lord Sugar and a room full of industry experts at London's City Hall. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is crowned the winner(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark explores the Welsh valleys in search of members for his choir, including a woman who was born in the Bahamas, and misses the strong sense of community(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A patient arrives in A&E with mysterious abdominal pains, and Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues, who has two wisdom teeth that need to be removed(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Hartlepool, PC Mick Johnson is stabbed in the arm as he tries to help a shopkeeper trapped inside a convenience store with a knife-wielding man, while a police officer in North Wales is knocked down by a driver trying to resist arrest. In Bradford, firefighter Phil Warden has his final bout of surgery following 10 years of treatment after a missile thrown through a fire engine window broke his nose in 11 places(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn travel to the small town of Deri near Caerphilly to celebrate the success of its rugby club, which has gone from the brink of extinction just four years ago to being crowned league champions. To mark the achievements of the players and the volunteers who run the club, Sam and Shauna cook up a huge venison feast with their help(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by Chris Bavin as they share a selection of festive recipes, including stuffed roast turkey and French toast with smoked bacon(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing head judge Len Goodman takes actress Lisa Riley - best known to Emmerdale fans as Mandy Dingle on a journey to Bronte Country - Haworth in West Yorkshire - to re-create a family trip there in 1992. The pair travel in a Citroen CX, just like the one Lisa's mum drove all those years ago, and after the obligatory Barbra Streisand singalong, Lisa explains the importance of the Bronte sisters on her childhood. Along the way, Len and Lisa take a trip on a steam trip, visit the Bronte memorial chapel and chat about the actress's career(n)

programme.title: Swallows and Amazons (1974)
programme.desc: Four youngsters go boating while on holiday in the Lake District where they encounter two more children with a boat of their own, and a rivalry develops over a series of escapades. Family adventure, based on Arthur Ransome's classic novel, starring Virginia McKenna, Ronald Fraser, Sophie Neville and Simon West(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford joins Si and Dave as they demonstrate festive recipes using leftovers, including Christmas pudding trifle, bubble and squeak, tartiflette, gougere, and turkey pie(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Monterrey v Liverpool (Kick-off 5.30pm). All the action from the semi-final at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Mexican outfit Monterrey prevailed 3-2 in an entertaining quarter-final against home team Al-Sadd, while Liverpool received a bye to this stage and will be expecting to go one better than in their previous appearance in this competition, when they finished as runners-up to Sao Paolo in 2005(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Porthcawl crew have just seconds to spare when two teenagers on holiday get caught on a rocky outcrop by a large swell and a rising spring tide, while in East Yorkshire, the Flamborough crew have to pluck a man from danger after he jumps off a 100 foot cliff for a dare. In Devon, the seas are often crowded with sailing craft of all shapes and sizes and when two collide, the Torbay lifeboat crew are called out to provide emergency medical assistance for a potentially life-changing injury(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the penultimate challenge, the four remaining chefs are challenged to create a single exceptional dish, inspired by a place that is special to them, as they fight to secure their place in the final three. After one contestant is sent home, the three remaining chefs travel to Lisbon to experience cooking alongside the acclaimed chef José Avillez. First, they take a trip to an historical food market, where they find ingredients to create their own dish for Chef José to taste, before joining José's brigade of chefs during a packed lunchtime service(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the families emerge from the first few hectic weeks with their new babies and realise that they need to re-evaluate their priorities. Paul and Craig strive to be truly equal parents, and Eilidh and Richard wonder whether they will ever get to take their baby home. Meanwhile, Mel returns to see the twins(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: The Blair Witch Project's Heather Donahue remembers the final year of the millennium, which saw her ultra-low-budget horror film terrify audiences the world over. Britney Spears shot to stardom with Hit Me Baby One More Time, director Baz Luhrmann offered the year's most curious entry into the pop charts, Sunscreen, and Channel 4 drama Queer as Folk arrived amid a storm of controversy. Kelly Brook began her short-lived stint on The Big Breakfast, Fatboy Slim's video for Praise You won critical acclaim and energy drink Red Bull outsold Coca-Cola as the millennium celebrations loomed(n)

programme.title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019
programme.desc: Christmas specials starring Diane Morgan as the incredibly stupid documentary-maker Philomena Cunk(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: TOTP 2 Presents the 90s
programme.desc: Steve Wright introduces a selection of archive studio performances and videos featuring the Spice Girls, Michael Jackson, Pulp, Shania Twain, Bryan Adams, the Prodigy, Madonna, Blur, Take That and others(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates visits Beijing, and learns about the life of President Xi Jinping and his attitudes on the relationship between Communism and capitalism. He discovers the Chinese social media platforms that have sprung up in the absence of banned Western sites and talks to some of the city's wealthiest and most impoverished citizens(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Summer arrives and with an increasing numbers of Brits holidaying at home, it looks like an ideal opportunity for Sainsbury's to cash in, especially in key Cornwall stores Newquay and Bude. However, Bristol depot manager Mike battles with traffic to deliver enough products for the holiday-makers, while in Penzance the fridges break down on one of the hottest days of the year. Meanwhile, at headquarters, product developer Charlotte is working on a vegan centrepiece for Christmas. Can she convince the press that her meat-free wellington is better than the competition?(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark arranges a singing flash-mob with a group of dog walkers to prove that shared experiences like singing can make people happy and feel more bonded(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dental director Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues who has two troublesome wisdom teeth that need to be removed in surgery(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace hosts the first week of the culinary contest, which sees the first six professional chefs competing for a place in this week's quarter-final. Half of the competitors are challenged by Marcus Wareing to prepare poached guinea fowl breast with a Sauce Robert, while Monica Galetti sets the rest the challenge of making hand-cut tagliatelle with a lamb ragu. Finally, they must prepare a signature dish to decide who will stay on and who will be eliminated(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by Anita Harris as they rustle up guinea fowl with carrot and parsnips, upside-down apple pie and warm stilton and walnut muffins(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes former EastEnders star Pam St Clement back to Dartmoor in Devon to relive a memorable period she enjoyed in the area in 1953. The pair visit the farm she stayed in as Pam explains how its owners - Courty and Sylvia - took her under their wings and became her extended family(n)

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Astrologer Russell Grant joins the duo as they reveal glamorous dishes able to impress any Christmas guests, including a recipe for turkey saltimbocca with game chips(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Lolly Adefope, journalist and presenter Dan Walker, presenter Sarah Greene and comedian Miles Jupp take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Lolly Adefope, BBC Breakfast host Dan Walker, presenter Sarah Greene and actor Miles Jupp continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Heston's Perfect Christmas Dinner
programme.desc: The chef and his research team try to create the ultimate Christmas dinner for Richard E Grant, Kirsty Wark, Rob Brydon, Terry Wogan, Sue Perkins and Dara O Briain. Heston travels to Oman, where he prepares a dish made from gold, frankincense and myrrh, and makes ice-cream out of reindeer in Siberia(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2019 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge. They are given three hours to cook a starter, a main course and a dessert, each of which takes their culinary abilities to a whole new level, with flawless presentation and exciting flavours. Judges Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Gregg Wallace assess their efforts, before deciding the winner(n)

programme.title: Heston's Marvellous Menu: Back to the Noughties
programme.desc: Celebrated chef Heston Blumenthal takes his world-famous restaurant the Fat Duck back to 2001, as restaurant critic Giles Coren challenges him to recreate an extraordinary tasting menu. Not only does this mean recreating pioneering recipes from almost 20 years ago, but he and his team also reassemble some of the original staff members to help recapture their glory days in the kitchen, while the dining room and kitchen are redecorated overnight to reflect 2001 styles(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This
programme.desc: Five celebrities go out for Christmas dinner together and between courses play a series of games, with the eventual loser having to pay for the meal. Taking part in this festive special are Strictly Come Dancing judge Shirley Ballas, comedian and TV presenter Joel Dommett, Love Island star Maura Higgins, MasterChef presenter Gregg Wallace and actor and funnyman Les Dennis(n)

programme.title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019
programme.desc: Christmas specials starring Diane Morgan as the incredibly stupid documentary-maker Philomena Cunk(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe gets the festive season under way with a selection of Christmas-themed hits from the show's archive, including performances by Tom Jones and Adele, and yuletide favourites by Wizzard, Slade, Jona Lewie, the Pogues and Kirsty MacColl and Shakin' Stevens. The show also features a recording of Pulp dressed up in winter attire for a rendition of their classic Britpop hit Common People, and a more traditional performance courtesy of Cilla Black(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create artwork for people they have never met, trying to work out their tastes by looking around their homes(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser's search for singers takes him to Cardiff's high street and offices, where he meets a young man from the countryside who is struggling to make friends in the city and a widow with a brilliant voice who has not wanted to engage with others since her husband's death(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Community nurse Jean is out on her rounds as a one-woman health service on the archipelago of Out Skerries, while the paramedics welcome a new recruit for maternity cover(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox heads to Somerset to help give equine dentist Victoria a leg up the love ladder. Victoria adores her country life, but the 35-year-old's dating pool is small and when it comes to men she is incredibly picky. Sara encourages Victoria to be more open-minded and she speed-dates five gents, before inviting her favourite three back to Somerset to sample rural life. Meanwhile, 24-year-old vet George is doing his best to woo the three ladies he's entertaining at his home in Hay-on-Wye, on the Welsh border(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on from Westminster and beyond, in the final programme of what has been a remarkably eventful year in domestic politics(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by actress Nina Wadia - Zainab Masood in EastEnders - as they share their culinary secrets, perfect for creating delicious festive flavours for Christmas. Mouth-watering dishes including succulent pork ribs with orange and pomegranate salsa, and pavlova with Christmas pudding ice-cream are prepared(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Mark Horton visits Liverpool to view Brunel's famous ship, the Great Eastern, which now lies beneath the mud. Mark recounts the story of her greatest triumph - how she conquered the Atlantic by connecting Britain's edge to America with 2,000 miles of telegraph wire(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of actor David Niven. The documentary highlights the film and television appearances he made over the years and captures the milestones of his fame, including his role as Phileas Fogg in Michael Todd's production of Around the World in 80 Days. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: The Bishop's Wife (1947)
programme.desc: Distraught bishop Henry is struggling to cope with building a new cathedral, but receives help from on high in the form of a suave guardian angel. The heavenly visitor proves influential in fundraising, but when he turns his attention to Henry's family life, the cleric suspects his new friend has designs on his wife. Fantasy, starring Cary Grant, David Niven and Loretta Young(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create an Austrian linzertorte, roast goose and all the trimmings, and a hazelnut chocolate brownie with clementine sorbet, as they find out how guest Aled Jones spends his Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun, there are also fantastic ideas for home-made edible Christmas presents(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Lolly Adefope, journalist and presenter Dan Walker, presenter Sarah Greene and comedian Miles Jupp test their general knowledge skills, with an overall champion being crowned for the week(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the last of three special editions of the quiz. This time, family teams the Lasletts, who appeared on the show in 2013, and the Meeples, from just last year, return to face off in a battle of lateral thinking and connections(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A calf born with an extraordinarily rare condition is brought in to see vet Martin Fitzpatrick at Castle Vets, where head nurse Julie Hurst advises the owners of seven-year-old cat Paws, whose weight has been steadily increasing. Vet Aidan O'Hare heads out to see Limousin bull Navarn, who has mysteriously gone lame. It is a blow to his owners because if he can't be successfully treated, he will have to be sold for meat at a serious loss. At the stud at Tullyraine Equine Clinic, breeder John books his mare Sweetbriar Rose to mate with stallion Conduit in the hope of producing a prize racehorse(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: One-off special in which Monica Galetti, Marcus Wareing and Gregg Wallace catch up with five finalists for whom taking part in the cookery competition has had a career-changing impact, taking them on a gastronomic journey that includes Majorca, the Scottish Highlands and the Devon town of Dartmouth. But it would not be MasterChef: The Professionals without an element of competition, so all five also return to the studio kitchen to show how much their food has changed since they first appeared - and try to prove that theirs is the best(n)

programme.title: A Merry Tudor Christmas with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian recreates festive traditions from the reign of Henry VIII. With the aid of food historian Annie Gray, she prepares two royal feasts, including a stuffed boar's head served by a choir, and the ancestors of the modern mince pies and Christmas cake. Lucy also gets a taste of the rabble-rousing anarchy of the Lord of Misrule, listens to carol singers performing a song written by Henry VIII himself, and watches a special royal performance involving dancing stags(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis mark the end of 2019 with a special show looking back on the year's events along with unseen material, out-takes and all the usual fun(n)

programme.title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019
programme.desc: Arguably 2019 wasn't the best year imaginable, but one thing most can agree on is that it happened. Here, Philomena Cunk, Barry Shitpeas and a galaxy of two other new faces look back at events. Comedy, starring Diane Morgan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Marianne and Leonard: Words of Love (2019)
programme.desc: The lives of composer Leonard Cohen and his Norwegian muse Marianne Ihlen, whose romance began on the Greek island of Hydra in 1960 as part of a bohemian community of foreign artists, writers, and musicians. This film follows their relationship, from the early days on Hydra, a humble time of 'free love' and open marriage, to how their love evolved when Leonard became a successful musician. Along the way the film tells of the tragedy that befell those that could not survive the beauty of Hydra, the highs and lows of Leonard's career, and the inspirational power that Marianne possessed(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the first semi-final as teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the academic quiz's festive edition(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Biologist Ben Garrod examines how animals use their skin to send out messages to their own species, and warning signals to others(n)

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold examine the festive season as it would have been celebrated in 1500 under the reign of Henry VII, concentrating on Christmas Day, New Year's Day and Twelfth Night. They make Tudor decorations, engage in festive revels and prepare feasting delights such as boar's head and shred pies - which were forerunners of mince pies and were made with meat. They also investigate the Tudor origins of Christmas carols, the singing of which was known as wassailing, and learn about the Lord of Misrule - traditionally a commoner placed in authority over his social betters for the festive period and in charge of directing the Christmas revelry(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Three-part documentary focusing on the monarch's final days. In the second episode, the country prepares for the unprecedented trial of the King. Although the consensus is that the King is a tyrant, only half of the commissioners appointed actually attend the meetings at Westminster Palace, in fear of being indicted as traitors. On the 20th of January 1649, the three day trial begins, and the King and the Lord President John Bradshaw become embroiled in a battle of wills(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic reveals his latest plastic surgery enhancement, three acts battle it out in Novelty Island, and Vaun tries to lure Vic away from Bob(n)

programme.title: House of Fools
programme.desc: It's Christmas Eve and Vic and Beef can't wait to get the party started, but Bob's celebrations are cut short when the special present he organised for his sulky son Erik is unexpectedly engulfed in flames. To find a suitable replacement, the housemates join Julie and Bosh on an underground boat journey to pop icon Phil Collins' mansion, while a final surprise awaits when Santa (Reece Shearsmith) arrives to save the day. Festive edition of the sitcom written by and starring Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, with Matt Berry, Dan Renton Skinner, Morgana Robinson and Daniel Simonsen(n)

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of Northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo(n)

programme.title: The High Art of the Low Countries
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon reveals how the entrepreneurial and industrious region of the Low Countries rose to become a cultural leader in the modern age, and despite its small size, managed to produce important forward-thinking artists such as Van Gogh, Delvaux and Magritte. He travels to the remote beach in north-west Holland that inspired Mondrian's transition toward abstract grid painting and examines the psychology and social history of the area(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas Shelby's long-awaited day has arrived, but in the middle of the celebrations, a mysterious visitor puts the entire Shelby family in danger, pulling the mob boss into a web of intrigue more lethal than anything he has yet encountered. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: On the orders of the secretive Economic League, Tommy goes to London to meet the wildly eccentric and volatile Duke Leon - and when he is given his mission, he soon realises the extreme lengths his new paymasters are willing to go to in their quest for power. Back in Birmingham, John's temper gets the better of him, and a personal dispute with the Italians threatens to explode into a devastating war. Tommy struggles to regain control of the situation - only to discover how extensive his new enemy's hold on the Peaky Blinders is. Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Joe Cole star, with an appearance by Paddy Considine(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy responds to the Italians' actions, setting him on a path of deadly vengeance that threatens to take him to his darkest place yet - and could also splinter the family. And when he meets Grand Duchess Izabella, the real force behind his mission, he discovers there's a traitor in his paymasters' midst - one who could unravel the entire plan. Meanwhile, Polly realises the depths of her new friend's intentions for her, and Linda implores an increasingly guilty Arthur to give up the criminal life(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the second semi-final of the academic quiz's festive series. Jeremy Paxman hosts(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Biologist Ben Garrod explores the armour and weaponry vertebrate skin has adapted to deploy, from quills to deadly toxins and camouflage(n)

programme.title: Wartime Farm
programme.desc: Historian Ruth Goodman and archaeologist Peter Ginn return to Manor Farm in Hampshire to recreate the conditions that prevailed in rural Britain during the Christmas of 1944. Peter calls upon crafts expert Colin Richards to help brew some improvised potato beer, while Ruth comes up with innovative presents for children and ingenious festive decorations made from scraps. Following in the footsteps of many wartime farmers, they transport their gifts, food and beer on a vintage steam train to Chislehurst Caves, where they discover what Christmas was like for some of the 15,000 people who sheltered there. Plus, Ruth cooks a yuletide meal from recipes and guidelines issued by the government and the Women's Voluntary Service(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: The king continues to publicly dispute the legitimacy of the high court but after two days of witness testimonies, the evidence against him is overwhelming. When Charles enters the courtroom on the third day, he is prepared to compromise but it is too late - he is found guilty of treason and is sentenced to be executed(n)

programme.title: The Story of A Fairytale of New York
programme.desc: Richard E Grant narrates a look at the making of the Pogues and Kirsty MacColl's classic bittersweet love song. The group return to the studio where the song was recorded, while director Peter Dougherty and actor Matt Dillon discuss the making of the video - which resulted in a brush with the law. With contributions from Jools Holland, Nick Cave and producer Steve Lillywhite(n)

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: A journey along America's West coast. The programme examines how the landscape is shaped by the Pacific Ocean as well as powerful forces beneath ground, particularly along the San Andreas Fault. The programme also visits the warm and sheltered waters of the Sea of Cortez, where animals including blue whales gather, and discovers how a small community in America's westernmost point have a unique relationship with grey whales(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy shares some important family news with his brothers, and outlines his plans for the most audacious criminal act the gang has ever undertaken. He also finds himself playing a dangerous game with Tatiana as they attempt to acquire information from each another. Meanwhile, a guilty Polly makes an ill-advised confession - with terrible consequences for her nephew. Cillian Murphy stars, with Helen McCrory, Paddy Considine and Gaite Jansen(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Russians test the Shelbys and their gang, and Tommy begins to realise he may have underestimated his new business partners, and has now been seriously outmanoeuvred. As aristocrats exiled by the Soviet Revolution, the Petrovnas have absolutely nothing to lose, and beneath their civilised veneer lies a fierce determination to regain what they have lost. However, the Birmingham mobster still has an ace up his sleeve, in the form of an old enemy now working as an ally. The question is, can he control him? Tom Hardy returns as menacing mobster Alfie Solomons, with Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory, Annabelle Wallis and Paul Anderson(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The third run of Steven Knight's period drama reaches its final episode, and yet again Cillian Murphy as Thomas Shelby finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation. Has his criminal life finally caught up with the Birmingham-based mobster? Tommy prepares to commit the most audacious crime of his career, only to receive an unexpected blow that puts him under unbearable pressure. In a nerve-shredding race against time, Tommy is forced to confront hard truths about himself and his family that could change the Peaky Blinders for ever. As he faces his worst fears he needs help more than ever - but is increasingly uncertain about who he can trust(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: The king continues to publicly dispute the legitimacy of the high court but after two days of witness testimonies, the evidence against him is overwhelming. When Charles enters the courtroom on the third day, he is prepared to compromise but it is too late - he is found guilty of treason and is sentenced to be executed(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as two teams each made up of four distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the final of the festive series of the academic quiz(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Wherever they live, skin is critical in allowing animals to sense the world around them. Even the toughest of animals, Crocodilians, have a surprisingly sensitive side when it comes to their specialised skin sensors they use to detect the tiniest of ripples in the water. Deadly Pit Vipers use heat sensors to ambush the small rodents they feed on. Ben Garrod puts them to the test with an experiment to see if they will strike a cold or warm ping-pong ball. He also uncovers how the leaf nosed bat puts its facial skin to good use as an acoustic lens to echolocate around its dense forest habitat(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Pet Shop Boys and Enya feature among the top hits and videos from 1988, while Cliff Richard sings Mistletoe and Wine, that year's Christmas Number one. Introduced by Bruno Brookes, Gary Davies and Anthea Turner(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Nicky Campbell present the edition first broadcast on December 22, featuring the Four Tops, a-ha, Kim Wilde, Shakin' Stevens, Freiheit, Neneh Cherry, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, Cliff Richard and Status Quo(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form from the mid-1980s to mid-1990s, an era that saw the success of 'New Traditionalists' such as Reba McEntire, George Strait and Dwight Yoakam. Garth Brooks exploded onto the scene to recalibrate the yardstick of success, while after being dropped from his label, an aging Johnny Cash, at the invitation of a hip-hop producer, returned to the studio with just his guitar and his unforgettable voice to record a series of albums that sold millions of copies and earned him the respect of the industry he helped to create. Contributors include Ricky Skaggs, Vince Gill, Willie Nelson, Rosanne Cash, Merle Haggard and Garth Brooks. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Christmas
programme.desc: Trisha Yearwood hosts and performs during this 10th annual Country Music Association's Christmas special. The event features festive classics, including one-of-a-kind collaborations by artists Lady Antebellum, Rascal Flatts, Runaway June, CeCe Winans, Brett Young and Chris Young. Plus, performances by Kristin Chenoweth, Chris Janson and Tori Kelly(n)

programme.title: Flat Pack Pop: Sweden's Music Miracle
programme.desc: James Ballardie charts Sweden's rise as a musical superpower, exploring the unique songwriting formula that inspired some of the biggest hits of the last 30 years. What started out as an experiment on the Stockholm underground club scene in the 1990s blossomed into a genre of its own, spearheaded by the likes of music producer and songwriter Denniz Pop. These unlikely heroes of bubblegum pop launched the careers of Britney Spears, the Backstreet Boys, Westlife and others. Featuring interviews with producers and artists, including Katy Perry, Justin Timberlake, Ace of Base and Robyn(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper advise teams at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, and Eric Knowles finds out about an ancient ferry service due to be reinstate(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi reels as his efforts to protest his innocence fall on deaf ears. Valerie begins to suspect Bear's motives as The Mill struggles to make sense of Jimmi's arrest(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in the Cotswolds on an unusual house-hunting mission with a couple whose £900,000 budget needs to fund two separate homes under one roof(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich help Steve and Alice in Exeter, who want an Ibiza-inspired space that also works as a family garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a much-loved teddy bear, a piano stool that is in desperate need of some specialist attention and a pair of art deco statuettes(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick's alternative nativity is under way but when he spots Linda drinking his concerns are clear. He confides in Sharon who promises to keep an eye on Linda. Over at the Arches tension reach breaking point as Jack desperately tries to convince Phil he has got the wrong man. Callum opens up to Lola about his concerns, but she is quick to praise him for how much he has changed Ben, and Kush visits Jean, but is baffled when he overhears something odd in Daniel's video message(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Bea requires emergency surgery, so Kian takes matters into his own hands, despite having been suspended - will this be the end of his career? Essie has a run-in with new consultant Ben Sherwood, just as Sacha encourages her to give him a second chance - although he may soon regret it. Dominic throws himself into work after receiving his divorce papers for Christmas(n)

programme.title: Geraint Thomas: The Road Will Decide
programme.desc: Documentary following the 2018 Tour de France winner as he battled to retain the title. The film captures the intensity of his journey as he negotiates 21 stages and 3,500km in 23 days, while facing extreme weather conditions, cancelled stages and competitors such as France's Julian Alaphilippe(n)

programme.title: Click and Collect
programme.desc: It is Christmas Eve and Andrew realises that he has failed to buy the one present his six-year-old daughter really wanted - Sparklehoof the Unicorn Princess. Unfortunately, it also happens to be the must-have toy of the season, and he cannot seem to find one on sale anywhere in London. So he and his over-friendly - and slightly irritating - neighbour Dev set off on a journey across the country as they try to track down the last remaining toy in the whole of the UK, which happens to be 300 miles away in Carlisle. Festive comedy, starring Stephen Merchant and Asim Chaudhry(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser raises the stakes by bringing all four of his choirs together to put on a festive grand finale in front of a large audience. He visits the performance venue and realises the scale of the challenge he has set the choir. He's concerned that he needs to fill the venue to make it the best experience, and hopes that the choir won't be overwhelmed. The grand finale performance culminates with a surprise reveal of some more musical friends for the groups to meet and carry on singing with. But not before a rousing Christmas sing-song, tinsel and all(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Halifax looking around a terraced house, as well as casting his eye over a Wolverhampton semi. Martin Roberts visits Ashford, Kent to see a former pharmacy(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E and the team members need to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland. Meanwhile, occupational therapist Marc Beswick returns to long-term patient Summer. The four-year-old has a condition that means it's hard for her to move her arms and legs. Marc has been working on her electric wheelchair, making modifications to make sure she can develop some independence(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time he is on a quest to discover all that is weird and wonderful(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for Christmas bargains at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while presenter Charlie Ross heads off to nearby Goldsborough Hall to learn about a special Royal Christmas gift sent to troops in the Second World War(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi realises he is facing a long stint in prison when his bail is revoked. Daniel gets to the bottom of Bear's ambilivance towards Jimmi(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple in West Sussex to find their first shared home together on a budget of £700,000, and meets a craftsman keeping the art of trug making alive. The presenter also finds time to meet master craftsmen keeping the county's traditional trug-making heritage alive(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Andy and Danielle have moved into a new home in Teignmouth, Devon, and are desperate for a garden that reminds them of their wonderful holidays in Morocco. Luckily for them, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich hope to make their dream a reality and design a Marrakesh rhiad-inspired back garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Caner Rachael South restores a wicker cradle that has been in use for generations. With a new addition to the family on the way the owner hopes to be able to pass this on to her grandchild and keep the heirloom alive. Meanwhile, Brenton West fixes a much-loved powder compact, made in the shape of a piano. Toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch add the finishing touch with a new, hand-sewn pink powder pouf and a wall clock is also rejuvenated(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat tries to charm Kheerat in the hope of keeping her home - and while he agrees, he increases their rent at the same time, which does not go down well with Mo and Jean. Sharon and Louise assume that Phil's bad mood is down to baby Peggy still being in hospital. Jay leaves Stuart in charge once more only to regret when he and Rainie cover the place in Christmas decorations(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Charlie Brooker assumes the role of host, with journalist and broadcaster Emma Barnett and comedian Phil Wang joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Taking a place on Graham's festive sofa, from the new blockbuster Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker are John Boyega and Daisy Ridley, alongside Superman himself, Henry Cavill, who is appearing in new Netflix fantasy drama series The Witcher. Ruth Jones and Rob Brydon, reunited for the much-anticipated Gavin & Stacey Christmas special, also appear. Plus, Robbie Williams performs a song from his new album The Christmas Present, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the influence of Wales' different performance traditions on the nation's comedy and on its biggest stars. He discovers the lasting legacy of the popular 70s double act Ryan and Ronnie on some of today's biggest performers, finds out how the folk club scene helped produce Max Boyce and sees how Rhod Gilbert has inspired a new generation of performers - including the latest winner of the Edinburgh comedy award Jordan Brookes(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The Priest takes Fleabag for some quiet reflection, leading to an unexpected revelation. After a day spent looking back on painful memories, she turns to him for solace, only to find more trouble. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, with Andrew Scott(n)

programme.desc: As the Kembleford Amateur Dramatics Society prepares to put on a pantomime, a grim incident from the group's past is unearthed. A body is found in the woods, and identified as a woman who went missing from a rehearsal seven years previously. An examination of the corpse provides evidence that a man who confessed to the murder was innocent, leaving Father Brown with a miscarriage of justice to put right in time for Christmas. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple in West Sussex to find their first shared home together on a budget of £700,000, and meets a craftsman keeping the art of trug making alive. The presenter also finds time to meet master craftsmen keeping the county's traditional trug-making heritage alive(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Andy and Danielle have moved into a new home in Teignmouth, Devon, and are desperate for a garden that reminds them of their wonderful holidays in Morocco. Luckily for them, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich hope to make their dream a reality and design a Marrakesh rhiad-inspired back garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Caner Rachael South restores a wicker cradle that has been in use for generations. With a new addition to the family on the way the owner hopes to be able to pass this on to her grandchild and keep the heirloom alive. Meanwhile, Brenton West fixes a much-loved powder compact, made in the shape of a piano. Toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch add the finishing touch with a new, hand-sewn pink powder pouf and a wall clock is also rejuvenated(n)

programme.title: Test Drive
programme.desc: Grado hosts a car-based game show in which three teams tour Glasgow answering questions via a specially-adapted satnav that will determine their route to a well-known tourist attraction. Correct responses mean they will follow the shortest route, but if they are wrong there will be diversions, wrong turns and miles added to their trip(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. programme.title: The Graham Norton Show

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark arranges a singing flash-mob with a group of dog walkers to prove that shared experiences like singing can make people happy and feel more bonded(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a semi-detached in Dover, Kent, and a parcel of land in Birmingham, and Martin Roberts explores the potential of a terrace in Tonypandy, south Wales(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dental director Dr Brian Chittick operates on one of his own colleagues who has two troublesome wisdom teeth that need to be removed in surgery(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, including a massage candle, a reusable hot drinks lid, and a board game. Lucy Alexander presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Anita Manning and experts Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw get into the panto spirit at Newark Antiques Fair for a festive edition of the show, helping the teams spend £300 on three items to take to auction in Derbyshire. Away from the contest, Anita joins a band for a seasonal sing-a-long and looks into the history of Christmas cards(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi is arrested when a stash of drugs are found in his car. Meanwhile, Daniel has to step in when he encounters a man who is gambling with his own life(n)

programme.title: The Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Part two of two. With police detectives seemingly no closer to closing the murder cases that have shaken Ballarat, their suspicion is aroused by some articles about unsolved cases in Ballarat - could these murders be linked to something long ago? Meanwhile, Jean's compassion is the only thing standing in the way of Matthew convicting the man who fell through the ceiling of the abandoned house. Australian mystery drama, starring Nadine Garner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman finds herself in the east Devon countryside with a couple who have £750,000 to find a more pastoral life and business. While in the county, Nicki goes deep into the woods to meet an artist who transforms fallen timber into beautiful bespoke winged sculptures(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry compete to design a family garden for a Cardiff couple, coming up with ideas that blend spaces for entertaining with room for two growing children(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts bring a 1930s portrait of Japanese martial arts master Yukio Tani back to life, rebuild a Victorian nursery chair and reconstruct an ornate porcelain clock(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Ahead of the final, Lord Sugar looks back on the tasks from this year's series and assesses the candidates' performances. The business tycoon also looks back on the boardroom bust-ups and goes through the best and worst decisions made by the contestants(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The two finalists take on their last challenge to win an investment from Lord Sugar, with contestants from the series returning to aid them in their endeavours. In an intensive three-day challenge, the finalists must create a new brand for their company, produce a digital billboard and make a television advert, before pitching to Lord Sugar and a room full of industry experts at London's City Hall. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is crowned the winner(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: Tom Allen meets this year's winner and runner-up, as well as Lord Sugar, to look back over the past 12 weeks of the business-based reality show. Featuring contributions from former contestants and special guests(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox. Ashleigh gets approved to buy a brand new house, and Sammyjo takes a big step in getting over Tom(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: In the final episode, Bev goes on the warpath as standards slip at the salon, and the Longlox girls look forward to Christmas and the future(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: This week, British pop royalty Ellie Goulding hands Michael her phone in Send To All, there is a sensational performance from the world's most beloved tenor, Andrea Bocelli, and two brothers from Coventry visit a relaxing spa but get the shock of their lives when they find themselves on stage at the London Palladium as they become the Unexpected Stars of the Show(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser's search for singers takes him to Cardiff's high street and offices, where he meets a young man from the countryside who is struggling to make friends in the city and a widow with a brilliant voice who has not wanted to engage with others since her husband's death(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores a property up for auction in Mountain Ash, south Wales, while residences in Gillingham, Kent, and Walsall, West Midlands are also examined(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Community nurse Jean is out on her rounds as a one-woman health service on the archipelago of Out Skerries, while the paramedics welcome a new recruit for maternity cover(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Neil hopes the customers will like his protein-rich snacks made of crickets and other insects. Plus, a portable cleaning device and a reusable sandwich wrapper(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper advise teams at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, and Eric Knowles finds out about an ancient ferry service due to be reinstate(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi reels as his efforts to protest his innocence fall on deaf ears. Valerie begins to suspect Bear's motives as The Mill struggles to make sense of Jimmi's arrest(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in the Cotswolds on an unusual house-hunting mission with a couple whose £900,000 budget needs to fund two separate homes under one roof(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich help Steve and Alice in Exeter, who want an Ibiza-inspired space that also works as a family garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a much-loved teddy bear, a piano stool that is in desperate need of some specialist attention and a pair of art deco statuettes(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick's alternative nativity is under way but when he spots Linda drinking his concerns are clear. He confides in Sharon who promises to keep an eye on Linda. Over at the Arches tension reach breaking point as Jack desperately tries to convince Phil he has got the wrong man. Callum opens up to Lola about his concerns, but she is quick to praise him for how much he has changed Ben, and Kush visits Jean, but is baffled when he overhears something odd in Daniel's video message(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Bea requires emergency surgery, so Kian takes matters into his own hands, despite having been suspended - will this be the end of his career? Essie has a run-in with new consultant Ben Sherwood, just as Sacha encourages her to give him a second chance - although he may soon regret it. Dominic throws himself into work after receiving his divorce papers for Christmas(n)

programme.title: Click and Collect
programme.desc: It is Christmas Eve and Andrew realises that he has failed to buy the one present his six-year-old daughter really wanted - Sparklehoof the Unicorn Princess. Unfortunately, it also happens to be the must-have toy of the season, and he cannot seem to find one on sale anywhere in London. So he and his over-friendly - and slightly irritating - neighbour Dev set off on a journey across the country as they try to track down the last remaining toy in the whole of the UK, which happens to be 300 miles away in Carlisle. Festive comedy, starring Stephen Merchant and Asim Chaudhry(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys D'Movie (2014)
programme.desc: The indomitable Irish matriarch discovers that a plan is afoot by developers to buy the famous Dublin street market where her family has run a fruit and veg stall for generations. Assembling a band of eccentric allies, she plans to fight back, only to realise her daughter is not interested in taking over the family business. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser raises the stakes by bringing all four of his choirs together to put on a festive grand finale in front of a large audience. He visits the performance venue and realises the scale of the challenge he has set the choir. He's concerned that he needs to fill the venue to make it the best experience, and hopes that the choir won't be overwhelmed. The grand finale performance culminates with a surprise reveal of some more musical friends for the groups to meet and carry on singing with. But not before a rousing Christmas sing-song, tinsel and all(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Halifax looking around a terraced house, as well as casting his eye over a Wolverhampton semi. Martin Roberts visits Ashford, Kent to see a former pharmacy(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E and the team members need to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on mainland Scotland. Meanwhile, occupational therapist Marc Beswick returns to long-term patient Summer. The four-year-old has a condition that means it's hard for her to move her arms and legs. Marc has been working on her electric wheelchair, making modifications to make sure she can develop some independence(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a hammock, a two-in-one colander and dish and a microfibre glove towel. Presented by Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past five years, a radical new church has been promising hundreds of young people an escape from gangs and crime and a pathway to salvation through prosperity and success. SPAC Nation, the Salvation Proclaimers Anointed Church, holds services in hotels, conference halls and football grounds and leader Tobi Adegboyega has been courted by politicians and the media for his work in tackling gang violence. But Panorama has discovered how the church's outward show of concern for young people may hide a less than charitable appetite for money. Reporter Greg McKenzie investigates its fundraising activities and speaks to young worshippers who say they've been forced into debt whilst church leaders enjoy lavish lifestyles(n)

programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. Lining up from the cobbles are Sue Nicholls (Audrey Roberts), Alan Halsall (Tyrone Dobbs) and Alexandra Mardell (Emma Brooker), while making their way from the Dales are Lisa Riley (Mandy Dingle), Isabel Hodgins (Victoria Sugden) and Nick Miles (Jimmy King)(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore talks to Piers about his career and personal life, including his spectacular fall from grace in the early 2000s when 31-year-old Stuart Lubbock was found dead in his swimming pool following a party at his home. Barrymore was never charged in relation to the death, but the controversy has dogged his career ever since and he has rarely been seen on television, besides a successful appearance in the 2006 edition of Celebrity Big Brother, where he finished as runner-up. He also talks about his close friendship with Princess Diana, the depths of his drug and alcohol addictions and the moment in which he came out as gay(n)

programme.title: Bancroft

Lining up from the cobbles are Sue Nicholls (Audrey Roberts), Alan Halsall (Tyrone Dobbs) and Alexandra Mardell (Emma Brooker), while making their way from the Dales are Lisa Riley (Mandy Dingle), Isabel Hodgins (Victoria Sugden) and Nick Miles (Jimmy King)(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore talks to Piers about his career and personal life, including his spectacular fall from grace in the early 2000s when 31-year-old Stuart Lubbock was found dead in his swimming pool following a party at his home. Barrymore was never charged in relation to the death, but the controversy has dogged his career ever since and he has rarely been seen on television, besides a successful appearance in the 2006 edition of Celebrity Big Brother, where he finished as runner-up. He also talks about his close friendship with Princess Diana, the depths of his drug and alcohol addictions and the moment in which he came out as gay(n)

programme.title: Bancroft

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A crime wave hits Ashfordly train station as a number of freight wagons mysteriously vanish, giving the police a tricky case to crack, but a sandwich could provide a vital clue. Peggy has plans to make some fast money when she decides to play dirty in the local bowls tournament, while a night out in Whitby proves eventful for Walker and Helen. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor, Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack finds Joe in the barn with the shotgun, Sarah goes into labour, the McAllister kids get a lecture on trust, and The Woolpack regulars plan the Easter fair(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Charlie's lorry is hijacked, and Ronnie takes Steve to Redcar and teaches him how to bet and not waste money. Denise is relieved when Ken tells her he is not looking for a wife(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson(n)

programme.title: The Bletchley Circle: San Francisco
programme.desc: When a Russian diplomat dies in Iris's arms, FBI officers sweep in to control the story and rule the death accidental. Iris is forced to get involved to protect her family. Meanwhile, Hailey befriends a world-renowned visiting cellist and all four women soon find themselves embroiled in a dangerous international firestorm. Drama set during the mid-1950s following female American cryptographers and female code breakers from the UK working to get to the bottom of a string of murders in San Francisco. Crystal Balint, Rachael Stirling, Julie Graham and Chanelle Peloso star(n)
