programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl is injured in a horse riding accident, leading the police to stumble upon a sinister series of animal thefts. Helen tries to tell Rob she doesn't want a relationship with him, but worries about hurting his feelings. Meanwhile, Oscar catches Peggy and David hosting a poker tournament in the Aidensfield Arms and bars them from the pub. Rural drama starring Sophie Ward, Derek Fowlds, Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank has plans to restore the village's sporting heritage. Kathy thinks about her future, and there is a shock in store for the Sugdens on a visit to the hospital(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah and Jack are confronted with an unexpected problem. Seth receives a proposition. Chris discovers what life at home will mean on his return from hospital(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel thinks Des is working too hard, while the police hunt for Charlie's rig and question him about the hijacking. Des struggles to hide his hatred of Alex at work(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is stunned when Ken tells her he has been seeing Denise, and she is pregnant. Derek looks forward to receiving his new company car, and Ronnie invites Steve to the races(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl is injured in a horse riding accident, leading the police to stumble upon a sinister series of animal thefts. Helen tries to tell Rob she doesn't want a relationship with him, but worries about hurting his feelings. Meanwhile, Oscar catches Peggy and David hosting a poker tournament in the Aidensfield Arms and bars them from the pub. Rural drama starring Sophie Ward, Derek Fowlds, Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the death of a pensioner found stabbed on a train at the height of rush hour. The case has a huge impact on Joe's home life when his daughter is named as a key witness, and he struggles to maintain a professional distance. The trail leads the team to a quiet Northumberland coastal town where clues appear to lead to one particular street - but why are the residents so reluctant to talk? Starring Brenda Blethyn, with David Leon, Sonya Cassidy, Jon Morrison and Eva Birthistle(n)

programme.title: The Bletchley Circle: San Francisco
programme.desc: While grappling with Jean's sudden disappearance, a death in the Big Bop Club brings an international incident right to the women's doorstep. Reeling from betrayals, Iris and Hailey work with Millie to plan a daring undercover operation to find Jean and expose Cold War treachery. Crystal Balint, Rachael Stirling, Julie Graham and Chanelle Peloso star in the final episode of this drama, set during the mid-1950s, following the lives of four remarkable women gifted with extraordinary intelligence, breathtaking capacity for pattern recognition, and a genius for decryption(n)

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: As the police try to piece together the fatal sequence of events, both women come under the spotlight, but have they got the right one? As the investigation spirals, Leah realises she must come face to face with Rose one last time, but it may be too late. Final episode of the drama, starring Katherine Kelly and Molly Windsor(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accidental shooting at Ashfordly Hall prompts Bellamy to investigate reports accusing acting estate manager Richard Bentham of exploiting youngsters for child labour, but his enquiries reveal a far more complicated situation. Peggy discovers a promising new source of revenue when she realises there is a market for guided tours of the area, but her excitement is cut short by a terrifying encounter. Rural drama, guest starring former Emmerdale actor Peter Amory, with Mark Jordon, Rupert Vansittart and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The McAllisters realise they have been put in their place. Seth recognises a piece of village history. Pollard takes a revived interest in art. Sarah comes to a momentous decision(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is determined to assert his independence, while Zoe begins to feel overlooked. The Sugdens face the awful truth together(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise is shocked when Ken admits he told Deirdre about the baby. Ronnie warns Steve that he could lose all his money. Bet asks Charlie if he had anything to do with the hijack(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz blames Jim for Steve leaving home. Deirdre is upset to see Ken and Denise drinking in the Rovers. Des agrees to see Tanya again as he can't stop thinking about her(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accidental shooting at Ashfordly Hall prompts Bellamy to investigate reports accusing acting estate manager Richard Bentham of exploiting youngsters for child labour, but his enquiries reveal a far more complicated situation. Peggy discovers a promising new source of revenue when she realises there is a market for guided tours of the area, but her excitement is cut short by a terrifying encounter. Rural drama, guest starring former Emmerdale actor Peter Amory, with Mark Jordon, Rupert Vansittart and Gwen Taylor(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called in when David Kenworthy, the son of a prominent local family, is found murdered on the beach at Whitley Bay. A witness spotted arcade owner Larry Crowe arguing with David on the night of the killing, and a search of the suspect's property uncovers a bloodied baseball bat. However, Larry has a cast-iron alibi and it's clear he wasn't the only person the victim met that night. A second death then leaves Vera facing mounting pressure to crack the case, and struggling to answer the question of why David was drawn to the seafront on the evening of his father's retirement. Brenda Blethyn stars in the murder mystery, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek starts at Hawthorne's, The Gazette runs a story on Jack, dubbing him The Bird Man of Weatherfield, and Deirdre is pleased to see Ken again(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita agrees to take Nick on as a paperboy, Deirdre is surprised to find Denise stand-offish, and Raquel suggests to Des that they entertain Tanya and Alex(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The McAllisters worry about Jess and her engagement. Eric's first wife Eileen shows up with some shocking news. Alan finds a couple who are touring the disaster site(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The gig at the Woolpack attracts a number of under-age drinkers. Michael sees Eileen going to Eric's cottage. Jack and Sarah arrive home to find their shotgun missing(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Clare finds herself under suspicion when a wild party ends in tragedy with a drugs-related death, and David is put in a tricky situation when his Aunty Peggy arrives in Aidensfield and sets up an illegal vodka still(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper discovers a cheap holiday scheme that requires the couple to join the Conservative Party - but George is a staunch Labour supporter(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers suffer a domestic crisis when Mildred discovers George has been writing to another woman. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A crime wave hits Ashfordly train station as a number of freight wagons mysteriously vanish, giving the police a tricky case to crack, but a sandwich could provide a vital clue. Peggy has plans to make some fast money when she decides to play dirty in the local bowls tournament, while a night out in Whitby proves eventful for Walker and Helen. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor, Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack finds Joe in the barn with the shotgun, Sarah goes into labour, the McAllister kids get a lecture on trust, and The Woolpack regulars plan the Easter fair(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank holds a Bank Holiday fair at the heritage farm. Jack and Sarah bring their new arrival home. Two young people find themselves drawn closer together(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick is annoyed when he has to spend the day babysitting. Des cannot bear the thought of Alex leaving his wife for Tanya. Denise swears to Ken she is not trying to tie him down(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Charlie's lorry is hijacked, and Ronnie takes Steve to Redcar and teaches him how to bet and not waste money. Denise is relieved when Ken tells her he is not looking for a wife(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A crime wave hits Ashfordly train station as a number of freight wagons mysteriously vanish, giving the police a tricky case to crack, but a sandwich could provide a vital clue. Peggy has plans to make some fast money when she decides to play dirty in the local bowls tournament, while a night out in Whitby proves eventful for Walker and Helen. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor, Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson(n)

programme.title: The Bletchley Circle: San Francisco
programme.desc: When a Russian diplomat dies in Iris's arms, FBI officers sweep in to control the story and rule the death accidental. Iris is forced to get involved to protect her family. Meanwhile, Hailey befriends a world-renowned visiting cellist and all four women soon find themselves embroiled in a dangerous international firestorm. Drama set during the mid-1950s following female American cryptographers and female code breakers from the UK working to get to the bottom of a string of murders in San Francisco. Crystal Balint, Rachael Stirling, Julie Graham and Chanelle Peloso star(n)

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: Leah deals with the fallout of Adam's betrayal, but some unexpected news further complicates matters. A distraction comes in the form of Michael and Angela's anniversary party, but as Rose's behaviour becomes progressively more volatile, the truth is finally exposed over the course of one explosive evening, with devastating consequences. Starring Katherine Kelly and Molly Windsor(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root(n)

programme.desc: Cash-strapped George knows exactly what he can sell to raise money - but decides not to tell Mildred. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called in when David Kenworthy, the son of a prominent local family, is found murdered on the beach at Whitley Bay. A witness spotted arcade owner Larry Crowe arguing with David on the night of the killing, and a search of the suspect's property uncovers a bloodied baseball bat. However, Larry has a cast-iron alibi and it's clear he wasn't the only person the victim met that night. A second death then leaves Vera facing mounting pressure to crack the case, and struggling to answer the question of why David was drawn to the seafront on the evening of his father's retirement. Brenda Blethyn stars in the murder mystery, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery(n)

programme.title: World of Sport: TV Firsts
programme.desc: Richard Henwood looks back at television sporting firsts, including footage of a 14-year-old Ronnie O'Sullivan who would go on to take snooker by storm(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: An important witness to a murder who objects to the Protectors' protection, vanishes soon after she is placed in the Protectors' charge. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A crook flees justice in the guise of a nun, hotly pursued by Simon Templar - who suddenly finds himself having to take a crash-course in the finer points of flying a stolen experimental plane. Drama, guest starring William Gaunt(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Strange Bedfellows (1995)
programme.desc: A horse trainer outraged by his brother's gambling debts murders both him and the bookie he owes money to, then claims one killed the other. Columbo is not convinced by his story and goes in search of the truth, and finds help from an unlikely source when a Mob enforcer is sent to investigate the bookie's death. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Rod Steiger and George Wendt(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur buys a large consignment of wine, supposedly brewed by monks. Before he can collect his order it is impounded, but he sneaks into a warehouse and retrieves it anyway. However, a disastrous tasting session at the Winchester reveals the product to be 'corked' and undrinkable and when he and Ray return it in the hope of a later refund they get locked in. George Cole and Gary Webster star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter uncover a plot to smuggle gold bars into the Middle East disguised as weights for an international strongman competition. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar poses as a jailbird in an attempt to learn who is responsible for organising a series of audacious mass prison escapes. Vintage drama, guest starring Donald Sutherland, with Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Murder of a Rock Star (1991)
programme.desc: The crafty detective pits his wits against a brilliant defence lawyer who has never lost a case. The attorney has murdered his rock star girlfriend to prevent her exposing his unethical courtroom tactics, and then framed her lover for the crime. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Little Richard, Dabney Coleman, Shera Danese, Cheryl Paris and Julian Stone(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's other half, 'er Indoors, disappears and he finds himself under suspicion of burying her on the forecourt in order to use the insurance money to prop up his dubious business ventures. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to prompt Arab cultural attache and assassin Rahad into carrying out a murderous deed, in the hope they can catch him red-handed just before he commits the act and force him out of the country. Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter investigate when a fellow officer is coerced into acting as a getaway driver by the gangsters who have kidnapped his wife. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman, and featuring a guest appearance by Billy Murray(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Jaws: The Revenge (1987)
programme.desc: Ellen Brody, the widow of heroic police chief Martin Brody, travels to the Bahamas after her youngest son is killed by a great white shark. But as she is reunited with her surviving son, the predatory fish sets off in pursuit to wipe out the entire family. Thriller sequel, starring Michael Caine, Lorraine Gary, Lance Guest and Mario Van Peebles(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

Thriller sequel, starring Michael Caine, Lorraine Gary, Lance Guest and Mario Van Peebles(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 2 (1989)
programme.desc: Headstrong cop Martin Riggs and his cautious partner Roger Murtaugh stumble across a plot by a South African diplomat to launder drugs money by shipping gold coins into the US via his embassy. Protected by diplomatic immunity, the crook looks certain to evade the law - while his chief henchman has a connection to Riggs' traumatic past. Action thriller sequel, with Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Joe Pesci, Joss Ackland, Derrick O'Connor and Patsy Kensit(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect a deposed African politician, who is due to attend a conference with a view to regaining power. programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: Michael faces a big day, as for the first time in his career, he is responsible for manoeuvring a 35-tonne plane into the correct position for take-off. Security supervisor Conor finds himself in a sticky situation with a padlock, and there is a look behind the scenes at the baggage hall, where 9,500 items of luggage pass through every day. Baggage agent Johnny is responsible for reuniting each lost item with its owner(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: The staff prepares for the grand re-opening of the airport after millions have been spent on renovations, and Phil nervously awaits a visit from Transport Secretary Chris Grayling. An oversized piece of hand luggage causes trouble at the gate, and problems arise for Special Assistance Agent involving three special charter pilgrimage planes(n)

programme.title: First Blood (1982)
programme.desc: Vietnam veteran John Rambo is wrongfully arrested in a backwoods town and tested to the limits of his endurance by a brutal deputy. Rambo escapes from his cell and heads out to the woods, and with the forces of the law in hot pursuit he finally has the chance to fight them on his own terms. Action thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Brian Dennehy, Richard Crenna and David Caruso(n)

Comedy horror, with Kevin Bacon, Fred Ward, Finn Carter, Michael Gross, Reba McEntire and Bobby Jacoby(n)

programme.desc: Captain McNeil's wife is kidnapped by a drug dealer who demands the police destroy all evidence of his crimes. A letter she left for her husband may hold a clue to her whereabouts(n)

programme.desc: The discovery of two mummified bodies in his girlfriend's new home sets alarm bells ringing for Quincy, who is convinced her eccentric neighbours warrant closer investigation(n)

programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton, Matt Tebbutt and Helen Lawal present tips on how to feed family and friend better, cheaper and faster at Christmas. They test three novel turkey-cooking methods against the clock - including deep-frying a whole bird - and share time-saving tips for preparing parsnips. They explore whether champagne is worth its hefty price tag, investigate whether digestif drinks really do help after a big meal and explore the psychology of why people cannot stop dipping into the chocolate box(n)

programme.title: My Grandparents' War
programme.desc: In the final episode, the actress Carey Mulligan discovers what happened to her grandfather who travelled thousands of miles to fight the Japanese in the final months of the Second World War. Her grandfather, Denzil Booth, lost his closest friends on board Britain's biggest warship, as it was the first to be attacked by a Japanese kamikaze. Later in the programme, Carey travels to Japan to understand the culture of the kamikaze from the other sid, and learns of the horrors her grandfather faced when he arrived in Tokyo(n)

programme.title: Father Ted

His bravery earns him the coveted Golden Cleric award, which is when his troubles really begin. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: Old People's Home for 4 Year Olds

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton, Matt Tebbutt and Dr Helen Lawal reveal surprises behind festive food. They include the fashion designer who pays for a professional pantomime company to perform in her home, the country mansion where Santa arrives by helicopter, the rocking horses that cost more than the real thing, the 24-carat-gold BMX bike and the colouring book setting its buyer back a cool £24,000(n)

programme.title: Autopsy
programme.desc: As a child star, Judy Garland won millions of fans playing Dorothy in The Wizard of Oz and went on to pack theatres and concert halls thanks to her extraordinary singing voice. However, away from the limelight she led a turbulent lifestyle and suffered a stormy love life. Not long after her wedding to fifth husband Mickey Deans in June 1969, her body was found in her bathroom. Judy's sudden death sparked rumour and speculation, and now forensic pathologist Dr Jason Payne-James examines her medical records to reveal exactly what caused the demise of this legendary Hollywood star(n)

programme.title: Autopsy
programme.desc: Dr Jason Payne-James re-examines the facts surrounding the sudden death of former Bee Gees band member Maurice Gibb, who collapsed and died in his home in Miami in 2003. Dr Jason Payne-James studies the autopsy report and events leading up to his collapse, in order to try and understand what caused the death of this musical icon(n)

On board Holland America's ship Nieuw Amsterdam, Jane goes backstage to meet the cast of the nightly musicals, has a cooking lesson from the executive chef and reveals how to make the most of a lazy day in port(n)

programme.desc: A seasonal special of the animal welfare series, which sees Inspector Lyndsey Taylor called to a desperate dog left home alone at Christmas. Can Lyndsey secure a warrant for police to enter the property in time? In Wirral, sorry-looking springer spaniel Max is signed over into Inspector Anthony Joynes's care, while Alan Davies joins vet Michael Lazarus at Putney Animal Hospital to x-ray the foot of Alfie, a Chihuahua who has developed a limp after leaping from his owner's arms(n)

programme.title: Freddie Mercury: A Christmas Story
programme.desc: Aside from being the only act in musical history to land the same UK Christmas number one twice, Queen's affinity with the yuletide season ran deeper than just commercial success. With Bohemian Rhapsody halfway through its nine-week run atop the charts in December 1975, Queen chose Christmas Eve to play a televised tour de force from London's Hammersmith Odeon. Freddie Mercury had a deep-rooted love for everything Christmas, with a lifelong generosity of spirit. In this documentary, friends and loved ones reflect on the band, alongside many of Queen's best-loved hits and rare footage of Mercury with some of his closest confidantes(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Hogans and Bucks swap homes and budgets for the week at Christmas time - and although they live only 13 miles apart, their financial circumstances differ greatly. Noel and Lisa Hogan live in a five-bedroom house in Farnham with daughters Aysha and Amber, and thanks to ex-policeman Noel's private investigation company, they have a weekly budget of £3,336. Meanwhile, Tanya Buck lives in a Basingstoke council house with her three kids. She owns a cleaning company and after bills is left with £140 to live on. How will they manage living each other's lives for a week?(n)

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: A power cut plunges most of the city into darkness, and Pride is approached by his daughter's best friend, Frankie, who claims to have received an unnerving message from her fiance Nick, who has gone missing. Using background noise from his message, the team deduces Nick's location, but finds he has been killed. A chain of evidence leads to an abandoned house containing blueprints for New Orleans' electric grids, as well as the murder weapon, and as Pride and his colleagues investigate further, they begin to suspect Nick's death and the blackout are connected to an opportunistic gang planning to use the power cut as a cover for a looting spree. However, there is more to the case than meets the eye. Crime drama, starring Scott Bakula(n)

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: A US Marine, a waitress and a chef are shot dead at a diner, in what is thought to be a bungled robbery. However, when the team arrives to investigate, a previously unknown witness emerges from the eatery's freezer, claiming the killer had an unusual tattoo. The statement rings alarm bells for LaSalle, who realises the circumstances of the killings match those of his first-ever case with Pride 10 years earlier, which remained unsolved after Hurricane Katrina devastated the crime scene. Convinced their killer has struck again, he and Pride work together once again in an attempt to achieve closure once and for all. Naval crime drama, starring Lucas Black and Scott Bakula(n)

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Georgie Barrat tests out the world's first VR bungee jump, while Jon Bentley heads back to primary school to see which dishwasher is best for removing congealed gravy and custard. Back in the studio, Craig Charles discusses money-saving tips with journalist Harry Wallop, while Jon, Georgie and Ortis Deley unbox one of the most exciting pieces of tech to hit the shelves this year(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Factories
programme.desc: In factories across the nation, manufacturers are hard at work ensuring that Brits everywhere have all the trimmings they need for a comforting classic Christmas. This documentary takes a look at how staples of the festive season actually get made, such as Christmas trees, Christmas crackers, tinsel, Christmas jumpers and Christmas puddings. Plus, a look at some food items, such as the 10,000 tonnes of sprouts grown and harvested in Lincolnshire and how enough figgy puddings are made in a single week to fill the seats of Tottenham Hotspur Stadium(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: In her final adventure in the current series, the singer takes to the Adriatic Sea. Starting in the popular and historic city of Dubrovnik, Croatia, Jane escapes the hordes of tourists on a four-wheel-drive tour in the picturesque hills of the city. Boarding the luxury ship, Jane's tour continues to Venice, where she meets gifted glass-makers on the island of Murano. The cruise then continues to the Roman city of Split in Croatia, before she ends her Adriatic adventure besides the mountains of Montenegro on the Bay of Kotor(n)

programme.title: Inside Sandringham: Holidaying with the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary charting every detail of the royal family's Christmas celebrations at Sandringham. Run with military precision and steeped in tradition, the film examines what Prince Harry's then-fiancee Meghan Markle has in store as she attends for the first time and the etiquette to which she must adhere. Plus, a look at the rich history of the royal family Christmas, going back to Victorian times. Contributors include royal insiders and cultural historians(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the famous gold-and-green facade of the London retailer, revealing the store's origins and the secrets of its success. Viewed as a uniquely British institution for more than 150 years, items on sale range from precious gems and high-class toys to designer fashions - meaning it remains a popular choice for shoppers wanting to indulge at Christmas. Former grotto staff share their secrets about what the super-rich might ask of Santa, while former employees reveal what it is like looking after celebrities and royalty during the festive season(n)

programme.title: Magnificent Obsession (1954)
programme.desc: A wealthy playboy blames himself for the death of the doctor who saved his own life in a boating accident, and when he later falls in love with the medic's widow and a second accident causes her to go blind, he trains as a surgeon in the hope of restoring her sight. Melodrama, starring Rock Hudson, Jane Wyman, Agnes Moorehead and Otto Kruger(n)

programme.title: The Poseidon Adventure (1972)
programme.desc: A luxury cruise liner at sea on New Year's Eve is struck by a freak tidal wave, which leaves it capsized and several passengers and crew trapped. A rebellious priest realises the ship is sinking fast, and leads a band of survivors up through the vessel in search of a way to the surface. Disaster movie, starring Gene Hackman, Ernest Borgnine, Shelley Winters, Leslie Nielsen and Roddy McDowall(n)

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora! (1970)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama recounting the bombing of Pearl Harbour from both the Japanese and American perspectives, reconstructing the events leading up to the devastating attack on the US naval base in 1941. Starring Martin Balsam, So Yamamura, Joseph Cotten, James Whitmore and Jason Robards(n)

programme.title: Only the Brave (2017)
programme.desc: Eric Marsh, the superintendent of a group of elite firefighters, is pushed to his limit when he and his crew tackle a historic wildfire. Fact-based drama, starring Josh Brolin, Jennifer Connelly and Andie MacDowell. Directed by Joseph Kosinski, helmer of Tron: Legacy and Top Gun: Maverick(n)

programme.title: Hampstead (2017)
programme.desc: An American widow finds unexpected love with a man living wild on Hampstead Heath when they take on the developers who want to destroy his home. Romantic comedy drama, starring Brendan Gleeson, Diane Keaton and James Norton(n)

programme.title: 47 Ronin (2013)
programme.desc: A band of samurai is betrayed by a ruthless warlord who murders the warriors' master and banishes them from their homeland. They receive fresh hope in the shape of a mysterious former slave, who leads them into battle against monstrous and magical enemies as they seek to reclaim their former status. Fantasy adventure, starring Keanu Reeves and Hiroyuki Sanada(n)

programme.title: Army of Darkness - Director's Cut (1992)
programme.desc: Zombie-battling hero Ash is transported back in time to the medieval era. In an attempt to find a way back to his own time, he accidentally summons up a horde of living skeletons led by his own evil double and must fight to send them back to the grave. Comedy fantasy adventure sequel to Evil Dead II, starring Bruce Campbell and Embeth Davidtz(n)

programme.title: Courage Mountain (1990)
programme.desc: The German army take over teenage heroine Heidi's boarding school on the eve of the First World War, prompting her to set off on a perilous expedition across the Swiss Alps to reach safety. Children's adventure, starring Juliette Caton, Charlie Sheen, Jan Rubes and Leslie Caron(n)

programme.title: Kidnapped (1971)
programme.desc: A naive young man is cheated out of his inheritance by his conniving uncle, who sells him to the captain of a slave ship. On board he forms an alliance with a hardened rebel, and the pair make their escape when the ship is wrecked on the Scottish coast. Period adventure based on Robert Louis Stevenson's novel set during the Jacobite Uprising, with Michael Caine, Lawrence Douglas, Trevor Howard, Jack Hawkins and Donald Pleasence(n)

programme.title: Crack in the World (1965)
programme.desc: A dying scientist and his colleagues conduct experiments to harness the Earth's inner energy that seem set to end in disaster. Sci-fi thriller, with Dana Andrews, Janette Scott, Kieron Moore and Alexander Knox(n)

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs(n)

programme.title: Behind Enemy Lines (2001)
programme.desc: A reconnaissance pilot is shot down and becomes stranded in enemy territory after filming evidence of a genocidal massacre by Serb militia in Bosnia. The naval air commander for Nato forces in the Balkans defies orders by mounting an unauthorised mission to rescue him, while the airman faces a battle to stay alive as he is pursued by paramilitary forces. Bosnian War drama, starring Gene Hackman, Owen Wilson, Gabriel Macht and Olek Krupa(n)

programme.title: Independence Day (1996)
programme.desc: A fleet of huge flying saucers launches a devastating and unprovoked attack on Earth. As survivors try to flee the seemingly unstoppable alien invaders, the courageous US president, an ace pilot and a computer genius come up with a plan to take the fight to the enemy and save the human race. Roland Emmerich's sci-fi adventure, starring Will Smith, Bill Pullman, Jeff Goldblum, Mary McDonnell, Randy Quaid, Robert Loggia and Harry Connick Jr(n)

programme.title: The Diary of a Teenage Girl (2015)
programme.desc: A girl growing up in 1970s San Francisco looks for a sense of certainty in a rapidly changing world. She finds solace in an affair with her mother's boyfriend, but also finds a personal refuge in developing her own artistic talents. Drama based on Phoebe Gloeckner's novel, starring Bel Powley, Alexander Skarsgard and Kristen Wiig(n)

programme.title: Top Secret! (1984)
programme.desc: A rock 'n' roll singer goes on tour in East Germany in the 1950s, where a scientist's daughter draws him into the world of international espionage through a French underground movement seeking to foil a Nazi plan to take over the world. Comedy, spoofing Second World War spy thrillers and Elvis movies, starring Val Kilmer, Lucy Gutteridge and Omar Sharif(n)

programme.title: Angels Sing (2013)
programme.desc: A man is forced to reassess his attitude to Christmas after meeting a cheerful, white-bearded, truck-driving stranger. Family comedy, starring Harry Connick Jr, Connie Britton, Willie Nelson and Chandler Canterbury(n)

programme.title: A Thousand Words (2012)
programme.desc: A spiritual guru decides to teach his lying literary agent a lesson for his dishonesty, and places a curse on him. The spell means he can only speak another 1,000 words in his life - and when the words run out, he will die. The only way to break the enchantment is to put right all his past mistakes. Comedy, starring Eddie Murphy and Kerry Washington(n)

Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jesse Eisenberg, Anne Hathaway and Jamie Foxx(n)

His cynical outlook begins to change when he falls for a single mother working toward her degree, while a talented pupil makes him consider what he wants from his life. Romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Marisa Tomei, Allison Janney and JK Simmons(n)

Jimmy Doherty finds out how Sri Lanka is coping with a looming coconut shortage, while Kate Quilton discovers whether compostables could be the answer to worldwide plastic pollution problem, annd Matt Tebbutt is in Paris to learn why the macaron has such a hefty price tag(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Andy and Caroline Carter love Florida so much they've visited around 20 times between them and spent their honeymoon there. They recently inherited some money, and their dream of buying a holiday home close to Orlando has become a reality. Ben Hillman shows them what their £130,000 budget will buy(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Retirees Peter and Glynnis Tabberer want to spend half of each year in Florida, close to Sarasota, so Ben Hillman shows them how far their £100,000 budget will stretch(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Charing, Kent, where landlady Natalie Palmer hopes to show that her B&B, The Oak, is better than any of her guests' establishments. Everything starts off well when a couple are delighted to have enough room for their yoga mats, but the rest of the party are unimpressed with the extractor fans(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the White Lady in Worstead, Norfolk, on their second night, where proprietor Dennis Gilligan tells his guests about a spooky presence that resides there. The guests get competitive in a round of clay shooting, and debate whether they believe in the existence of ghosts(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Cranleigh House B&B in Combe Martin, Devon, where former couple Katherine Armitage and Stewart Johnston offer alternative accommodation for those who enjoy yoga and are seeking spiritual enlightenment. They hope the group will send out energy for world peace and healing, but Mike and Denise question whether the whole thing is a joke(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike and Denise Mitchell welcome the contestants to Beaches B&B in Paignton, Devon, which only opened for business a few weeks ago. Fellow competitor Katherine puts the owners through their paces at breakfast when she asks for something off the menu, and the hosts are pleasantly surprised at feedback time(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together for one last time to discover what they have been paid, and the identity of the winner. The Oak owner Natalie wants to know why some of her guests were not enthused by her extractor fans, but she herself receives aggression from Dennis over her criticism of the White Lady. Katherine and Stuart defend their vegetarian breakfast, and Mike and Denise deal with negative comments about their cereal selection(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Personal trainer Eve Booth hosts the first dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, expressing concern that her starter of haggis and black pudding may not be to everyone's taste. Although her main course gets a lukewarm reception, the biggest threat to the evening seems to be fellow contestant Ken Brown's taste in jokes(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer and gypsy wedding supplier Paula Thompson hosts the second party in Co Durham and Wearside, and hopes to bring a touch of class to the proceedings with her 'black and bling' themed event. The evening's menu includes fresh lobster and hairy prawns, accompanied by a spot of belly dancing(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old languages student Donal Anand-Shaw hosts the third dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, and decides to make the event black tie. He also arranges for a string quartet to play, and serves up a locally-sourced scallop starter(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Debt collector Eleri Watson hosts the fourth dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside. However, her efforts to organise an all-Welsh evening do not go according to plan when soil is found in the leek soup and lamb is served burned, dampening the guests' enthusiasm(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businessman Ken Brown hosts the final dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, and hopes to entertain his guests with a 'wear a wig' themed evening. After annoying his fellow contestants over the past week, he hopes to make amends with his unique entertainment, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Special edition of the documentary which gets up close and personal with the animals in Chester Zoo's aquatic habitat. Rare blind cave fish Tiny Tim needs a love potion to help him become a father, and Popeye the chalk bass - the toughest fighter on the reef - needs an urgent operation after injuring his eye in an altercation. The zookeepers hope to breed from Ophelia the Mexican lake salamander, of which there are only 100 females left in the world, while young penguin Frazzle needs to find a mate and settle down to family life. But he cannot seem to stop flirting with every female in sight(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Festive edition of the documentary from 2015 following the work of Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team. A sleigh-pulling Alaskan malamute needs both knees replacing with implants, but his owners are forced to make a difficult decision as he recovers from his first operation. Meanwhile, a springer spaniel is rushed into the surgery with a shattered jaw, so the vets decide to try something new to get him home in time for Christmas. Plus, a miniature dachshund with a deformed foot is given intensive physiotherapy, and Noel gets a surprise visitor(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics help a 24-year-old man who is having an epileptic seizure in a supermarket, while an ambulance crew treats an elderly woman whose nose will not stop bleeding(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform(n)

programme.title: The Supervet

Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent takes her usual place in Dictionary Corner with David O'Doherty, while mathematics whizz Rachel Riley is on hand to provide the letters and numbers(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Old Times Guesthouse in Ipswich, where mother-and-daughter duo Linda and Kelly Langdown hope a go-karting session will get the contest off to a flying start. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit the Flower Pot in Sunbury-on-Thames on their second night, where owner Simon Bailey and French manager Pierre Cudon provide stylish continental-inspired accommodation, but the hosts are left puzzled by some of the feedback they receive the next morning(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the family-run Dolphin Hotel in Exmouth, Devon, which has been a life's work for hotelier Jo Knowles. On arrival, Pat and Nigel find a suspicious stain, before the guests put on their war faces for a game of paintball. Jo takes everyone to a floating restaurant for dinner, and the next morning she presents a huge spread for breakfast - but her diners are suspicious that it has been laid on specially for the competition(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants stay at the Mariners Beachside B&B in Seaton, Devon, but are disappointed by the cleanliness of the property. They take part in a town-crying competition during the afternoon, and host Nigel feels under pressure to deliver his award-winning breakfast the next morning. Come feedback time, the owners discover not all their guests share the same opinion(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Linda and Kelly Langdown of the Old Times Guest House question the rating of their facilities, while the Dolphin Hotel's Jo Knowles has a confession to make regarding her hosting(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Charity manager Andrew Batsford hosts the first of a week of parties from Hastings, aiming to inspire his fellow contestants with a menu of dishes prepared using foraged ingredients, including sea broccoli and stinging nettles. However, if the food fails to win them over, he hopes a dynamic Wham!-themed evening of festivities will do the trick(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the second day of the competition in Hastings, East Sussex, and retired solicitor Adrian Carter is hoping to impress his guests with a Moroccan-themed evening. However, disaster strikes when he neglects his tagine, and his rendition of a Rod Stewart classic does not go down well either(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sally-Anne Keller hosts a German-themed dinner party on the third night in Hastings, East Sussex, and requires her guests to dress, sing and dance accordingly. Charity manager Andrew Batsford explains how the principles of anarchism and punk can be applied to football, while retired solicitor Adrian Carter dresses as the 19th-century statesman Otto von Bismarck and raises eyebrows with his jokes(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Kate Eadon hosts the fourth dinner party in Hastings, East Sussex, with a menu styled on a gourmet pub meal. Adrian Carter gets himself in hot water, Sally-Anne Keller reveals a surprise celebrity crush, and the host recounts her own encounter with a famous funnyman(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The confident Stelios Michael hosts the final night in Hastings, East Sussex, hoping to impress his guests with a Greek-inspired evening. The diners get involved when they receive a masterclass in the country's traditional dance, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: With Christmas fast approaching, the keepers try to get the animals into the festive spirit. Stockings have been hung for the Sumatran orang-utan family, only for eight-year-old Indah to open everyone else's. There is concern about the health of reticulated python Bali, who has been off her food for more than three months. She needs a check-up, but at 23ft it takes 10 brave keepers to catch her. Malayan tapirs Marjorie and Bitong welcome a baby, however the new dad is not happy when his daughter comes between him and his partner, and sun bear couple Tony and Millie's relationship is growing stale - will a trial separation help?(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Festive edition of the documentary following the work of Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team. Charity worker Kerry brings in Maxi, a three-year old rescue greyhound with severely damaged front legs. Noel's dog Keira is rushed into the practice with sickness and diarrhoea. The team also treats River, a Border terrier puppy who has fractured both back legs, and Noel makes a surprise visit home to Ireland(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedic Rob and technician Mike rush to a 21-month-old baby who has fallen unconscious in Kidderminster, while two men collapse in a pub in Coventry(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Marie, 31, arrives at St George's A&E in south-west London by ambulance after collapsing at home with an unexplained headache. Doctors work fast to ascertain the cause of her collapse, with potentially devastating results. Marie's joined by her partner Cameron and her young child. Cameras also follow Eileen, 87, who comes in with shortness of breath and a high heart rate, and 10-month-old Erin, who has a build-up of wax in her ears following a cold and fever(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max works to solve the underlying problems at the hospital despite his continuing struggles with treatment. Reynolds chooses to perform a costly surgery to determine an elusive diagnosis, and Bloom is forced to face her personal demons(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Owners Richard and Jorge's in-fighting has alienated locals, and the duo's suggestion that customer numbers are low because of racism has heightened tensions(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max works to solve the underlying problems at the hospital despite his continuing struggles with treatment. Reynolds chooses to perform a costly surgery to determine an elusive diagnosis, and Bloom is forced to face her personal demons(n)

programme.title: Animal Airport
programme.desc: Five rescued puppies from Dubai push the team to the limit, while supervisor Shannon has to track down a missing shipment of reptiles from Indonesia(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton learns about a potentially life-saving property of discarded shrimp shells and Matt Tebbutt investigates why people cannot seem to get enough of salted caramel. Plus, Jimmy Doherty explores the price differences between fresh and frozen pizzas in supermarkets(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Alex and Jacqui MacPherson, from Eastbourne, East Sussex, plan to head to warmer climes to enjoy their retirement. They're looking for a home in the Tampa area of Florida where they can spend the winter months, so Ben Hillman shows them five properties for their budget of £80,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Having inherited some money, Hampshire couple Phil and Bernie Smith are now in a position of being able to afford a holiday home. They have decided that south Tenerife's warm winter climate is ideal for whenever Phil's work as a gardener in the UK slows down, so Scarlette Douglas shows them five apartments within their £100,000 budget for them to choose between(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Borgue Hotel in Dumfries and Galloway, which owners Vince and Shirley Barrett run with military precision. On arrival, guests Adam and Naomi are not impressed with the decor, while Ken and Elaine do not like the orange towels. Later, over dinner, the hosts reveal who wears the trousers in their relationship, before Ken's jokes go down like a lead balloon(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Allscott Mill B&B in Telford, Shropshire, on their second night, where hosts Ken and Elaine Purchase welcome their guests. On arrival, Shirely and Vince find fault with the lack of headroom in their lodgings, while Adam and Naomi are bemused by the absence of a toilet brush in their bathroom(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Park House in Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where owners Ian and Joan Barr hope their colourful B&B will impress their guests. Adam and Naomi are wowed by the vibrant decor, but Ken and Elaine take issue with a dirty toilet brush(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

They are pleased to discover it is Vince and Shirley's dream location, but Ken and Elaine don't share the same opinion. Later, the guests take part in an archery competition, and in the morning, are unimpressed with the DIY breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival owners meet for the final time on the last day of the competition to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Hosting skills, breakfasts, facilities and two toilet brush gripes are aired before the cash is counted and the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: NHS manager Helen Mather holds the first party in Leicester, hoping her plans for a simple menu will allow her more time to entertain her guests. However, the accident-prone host has a mishap involving raw pig's blood, and tries to turn the evening around with some 'urban ballet' after one diner sours the mood with a display of less-than-polite table manners(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Terry Robinson hosts the second dinner party in Leicester with a 'Dress as Your Hero' theme, and the Elton John fan also decides to treat his guests to a song. He is confident his menu will score him top marks, but while Richard is blown away, Ryan remains unimpressed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty spa manager Porchia Lee hosts the third dinner party in Leicester, serving up a French-themed menu of chicken liver pate, pan-fried chicken with tarragon sauce and creme brulee. Unfortunately for her the pudding is so bad her guests are reduced to tears, and double glazing salesman Richard Deeping insults her appearance(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT enthusiast Ryan Bruce hosts the fourth dinner party in Leicester, and tries to impress his guests with a Spanish-inspired menu cooked in front of them. Entertainment is provided by Ryan's sister, West End actress Charlie Bruce, who puts the contestants through their paces in a salsa lesson(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Double-glazing salesman Richard Deeping hosts the final dinner party in Leicester, and hopes to make up for his boisterous behaviour by impressing the guests with his culinary prowess. In a bid to land higher scores, he puts on his own brand of entertainment, only to reveal a little too much of himself in the process, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester Zoo's keepers are busy making gifts for all the animals, but with chimp leader Dylan otherwise occupied by female attention, it is a chaotic free-for-all when the festive parcels are handed over to the chimps. Meanwhile, rhino couple Beni and Asha are experiencing family dysfunction, and when Asha ignores Beni, he is left to find solace in the company of a deer. Elsewhere, The zoo's new thousand-strong family of leaf cutter ants work together to build a new home for themselves out of their Christmas bounty(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick enters new territory when he is called upon to help a Humboldt penguin chick. Wall-e is from Longleat Safari Park in Wiltshire and needs an operation to cure his unhappy foot. He also treats a three-legged Maine Coon cat that needs help with a mysterious swelling in its back leg and a husky from Thailand undergoes hip surgery(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Series following an hour in the life of ambulance crews in the West Midlands, starting with paramedic Chelsie Kennedy and technician Simon Lees rushing to save a baby, who they fight to resuscitate while rushing him to the nearest baby care unit. In Birmingham, paramedics Jo Hardwick and Peter Bowles treat a five-year-old boy who is having an asthma attack; and, across the city, student paramedic Dave Watts must attend his first cardiac arrest when an elderly woman is found in her bed without a pulse(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Programme following Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree through the process of buying and restoring a French chateau that has been empty for the past four decades. Their new home has 12 acres of land, nine outbuildings, a walled garden and a moat - but it also has no electricity, heating or water, and proves to be a far more complicated DIY project than expected(n)

programme.title: The Team
programme.desc: To find the links between the trade in looted art and terrorism, the team works back through the chain of intermediaries and starts with the weakest link - Tebos Meyer. Back in Denmark, Malu seeks help from the police as the Syrian government exerts political pressure on the Danish authorities and demands Malu's extradition and the return of The Garden of Ishtar. Despite all this, Malu is determined to escape and take the artefact to a safe place. Crime thriller, with Jurgen Vogel. In English, German, Flemish, French and Danish(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cyclist Stephen, who's 48, has severely damaged his ankle after coming off his bike deep in woodland in Surrey, and doctors treat a man rushed in after collapsing with a suspected stroke in his garden. Meanwhile, Bridget, who's 70, broke her wrist when she slipped on a mountain path in Greece(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gary, 50, is brought into St George's hospital by ambulance after a garage collapsed and fell on his legs. His wife Peta joins him, while Maggie, 83, comes in to A&E with suspected sepsis. She's joined by her son and daughter, who also deal with the fact their mother has lung cancer. Plus, cameras follow one-year-old George, who is rushed in with his mother after drinking nail varnish remover(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James concludes his examination of the social significance of cars by exploring how aspiration and new wealth were behind the development of some of the greatest models ever made. He tries to make sense of the baffling world of company car hierarchy by holding a travelling salesman race-off and indulges his 1980s urges with the twin delights of a Lamborghini and a Porsche. He also unveils his choice of the ultimate people's car - a vehicle that can lay claim to being the greatest in history(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys visit a collection of classic cars in Colorado, including a 1961 Messerschmitt, and decide to make an offer on a prize exhibit(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees UK bidders battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages in Kent(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees bidders battling to win potential treasures found in Yeovil's storage containers, lock-ups and garages. Linda has to work for her profit and tensions reach boiling point as Dan pushes Nat to her limits(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The lives of Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz, two of America's most skilled 'pickers', who travel around the country trawling junk yards, garages, attics and barns for precious objects. The guys unearth ultra-rare skis for a Model T Ford as they pick one of the biggest barns they've ever seen(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Frank makes a shrewd bid that leads to a big discovery and Mike falls hard for two century-old cars(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and David Baddiel travel along more than 1,200 miles of hazardous roads in Ethiopia on a journey to Aksum, the country's holiest place. However, they have to contend with more than fatigue and unreliable terrain to reach their destination, as they encounter drug-taking truck drivers, suffer a disastrous breakdown and start to run out of fuel at the worst possible time(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond debate which company has made the greatest number of exceptional vehicles, and James May finds a way of transporting caravans to campsites without inconveniencing other road users. The Top Gear track plays host to a replica of one of the world's most famous rally cars, and broadcaster Chris Evans - some five years before he was announced as the show's new presenter - is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Timber Kings
programme.desc: Joel takes on an epic build and Peter travels to a historic gold rush town to build a mining shaft house that would make any prospector proud(n)

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: John and Dave get rid of unwanted skips and trailers, which are home to some unusual objects. Elsewhere, emergency repairs are required to get two police bikes back out on the road(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Renaultsport Twingo 133 through a stringent examination in Belfast - with a helping hand from actor Ross Kemp. Richard Hammond samples the delights of racing airport vehicles, and James May takes the Vauxhall Insignia VXR for a drive in the company of Margaret Calvert, whose ideas were central to the design of modern British road signs. Film director Guy Ritchie is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and there is also a group test of high-performance off-roaders from BMW, Audi and Range Rover(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Strongman Eddie meets - and eats - gators roaming the Everglades, tests his grappling skills with WWE legends The Headbangers, and tries to devour a kitchen sink full of ice-cream(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie visits the Garden State for supersized breakfast in a New Jersey diner, a tryout with ice hockey pros the NJ Devils - and an epic struggle with an eight-hot-dog challenge(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the Christmas fun are former Test Match Special commentator Henry Blofeld, Witless star Kerry Howard, broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles and BBC News presenter Clive Myrie. They all reveal amazing stories, but are they telling the truth, or making it all up?(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Panellists John Sessions, Mark Steel, Clive Anderson and Alan Davies answer questions on corks, cats and carbon dioxide, with host Stephen Fry awarding points for the answers he finds most interesting(n)

programme.title: QI

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster joins a team of historians and geologists to recreate a pioneering 280-mile journey through the natural wonder that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Following in Powell's footsteps in the stunning yet challenging north Arizona landscape, Snow and the crew take on the area's rapids in antique wooden boats as they learn about the region's geological and Native American past(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The comedian begins the next leg of his trip in Kasama, northern Zambia. His first stop is the town of Mpika, where he meets college students training to be locomotive drivers. Next, in the capital Lusaka, he reveals how the first railways were built to access the country's rich mineral wealth, and watches craftsmen cutting emeralds and rubies - before being let loose on a pair of bejewelled cufflinks. Griff then reaches the spectacular Victoria Falls on his way to Zimbabwe's second-largest city, Bulawayo(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and a team of specialist historians help Hektor Rous, the son of 'Aussie Earl' Keith Rous, piece together the mysterious history of the family's Tudor country home in Suffolk. In 2004, he moved from Melbourne, Australia, hoping to rebuild the estate's fortunes, and established events such as the Latitude Festival in its 3,500-acre grounds(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and his team head to an army training ground on Salisbury Plain to discover what lies beneath the Barrow Clump, a huge mound of earth built by Bronze Age people to bury their dead. More than 2,500 years later, the Anglo-Saxons re-used the location as a burial site, leaving behind some fascinating artefacts. The team is joined for the dig by a group of wounded soldiers whose members are being trained in archaeology as part of their rehabilitation(n)

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: One of the largest Nazi bunkers ever constructed, outside the French town of Watten, a missile facility with storage for 108 missiles, a liquid oxygen factory and two launch pads(n)

programme.title: Viking Dead
programme.desc: How a well-preserved Viking ship discovered in 1904 continues to present archaeologists with new mysteries to unravel(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the emperor penguin and the wood frog, both of which survive in conditions so cold they seem to defy the laws of nature. The emperor is the only penguin able to breed on Antarctic sea ice during winter, while the wood frog can survive up to 65 per cent of its body mass freezing during its hibernation(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines creatures that spin and weave, contemplating the history of silk production and its romantic origins in China(n)

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: An exploration of how invertebrates' production of silk 300 million years ago became their greatest invention. Stronger than a steel thread of the same diameter, the material is as pliable as elastic and employed by spiders for activities as diverse as courtship, catching prey and tending their young. David Attenborough reveals how some communal spiders create silken palaces rising 15m to 20m into the forest canopy(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to the Lake District to look for copper, a forgotten piece of the nation's industrial heritage. There are nearly two dozen old mines across the mountains, some thought to have been established 400 years ago when the valleys would have been studded with workshops, scaffolding and water-powered machines, home to a brave band of Tudor miners. The team battles the rain, wind and dangerously unstable trackways, but the combination of sheer effort and hi-tech kit enables the experts to access the heart of an old mine(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The team heads to the small county of Rutland to visit Oakham Castle, the most well-preserved 12th-century building in Britain. It was once home to the Norman knight Walkelin de Ferrers, who fought alongside Richard the Lionheart in the Crusades, and before that the residence of several Saxon kings and queens. Tony and the crew investigate de Ferrers' long-lost private quarters, but it takes three days of twists and turns to determine what the site looked like 900 years ago. To mark the visit, they also cast a giant Time Team horseshoe to add to the collection that has decorated the castle hall for half a millennium(n)

programme.title: Deadliest Crash: Disaster at Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary examining what caused Pierre Levegh's Mercedes to crash into the crowd during the 1955 Le Mans 24-hour race, killing more than 80 people - an accident that remains the worst in the history of motor racing. Among the theories discussed are the use of untested technologies by the car's manufacturers, the addition of a lethal fuel additive and allegations of reckless driving by future Formula One world champion Mike Hawthorn, who won the race with Jaguar team partner Ivor Bueb. Narrated by Jack Deam(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the comedian crosses 1,000 miles of South Africa, reaching the most southerly passenger railway station on the continent. Heading to Johannesburg, he reveals how the city has benefited from the scale of gold-mining in the area. Later on, he takes to the skies in a light aircraft for a bird's-eye view of one of South Africa's largest ostrich farms, and catches one of the world's most luxurious trains for a seven-hour ride to Cape Town, before reaching the coastal settlement of Simon's Town(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The experts head to a field in south Oxfordshire where significant findings were made by an inquisitive PhD student half a century ago, including a mosaic and the stone walls of a former Roman building. Tony Robinson and the crew examine aerial pictures showing clear building lines in the ground, and speak to the current landowner, a farmer who continues to uncover bricks and tiles. They try to evaluate the building's purpose and how the Thames may have shifted the remains over the course of two millennia(n)

programme.title: Time Team

Cameras also capture the sheer horror of being swept overboard by a monster wave in the middle of the night(n)

Less lucky in love are 21-year-old Fran from Milton Keynes and 45-year-old Richard from Surrey - but nevertheless, both are planning to return to the dating fray(n)

The patients include a shih tzu with damaged discs that are treated with special supports, small pins and cement, a bulldog puppy with a fracture that extends across a growth plate, and a German shepherd that has had a leg amputated, and has developed a hip problem from walking on three legs(n)

programme.title: Christmas at Chatsworth House
programme.desc: One-off documentary going behind the scenes of Chatsworth, one of England's most impressive stately homes, as the Duke, Duchess and their 800 staff brace themselves for an influx of 200,000 visitors over the 2019 festive season. Christmas is the most important event for the Derbyshire estate and crucial to its survival. In just five days, the house is transformed with 30 Christmas trees, 60,000 baubles, and a million fairy lights. But this year Derbyshire has been crippled by unprecedented flooding. Can family and staff rally to make this a season to be remembered?(n)
