programme.desc: Niles and Martin take pity on Frasier and individually set him up on dates with three women who they believe will sweep him off his feet, but unfortunately things do not go to plan. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole visit the Cotswolds, where they unearth a mystery item that looks like a fish tank but turns out to have a very different use entirely(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin draws on his property expertise to find Dorset couple Andy and Barbara the perfect holiday home in Benidorm on a budget of £150,000. The couple have been together for over 40 years, but after a health scare for Andy, they've decided to seize the moment and buy a bolt-hole in Benidorm, Spain(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Christmas
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp oversees the fun as four craft enthusiasts create Christmas stockings. Plus, a sweet crafter who sculpts with sugar and two cheap-and-cheerful mini makes(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A special festive edition following Noel Fitzpatrick as he treats former rescue dogs who have found a new life as family pets, but are still plagued by medical problems. The patients include a shih tzu with damaged discs that are treated with special supports, small pins and cement, a bulldog puppy with a fracture that extends across a growth plate, and a German shepherd that has had a leg amputated, and has developed a hip problem from walking on three legs(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 71-year-old beekeeper is looking for a man with a naughty side to his nature, while a 21-year-old is reluctant to venture from his house, which has dented his chances of romance. Other visitors to the restaurant include a charity shop manager who has his wedding all mapped out and a Welsh hairdresser recovering from a string of failed relationships, who spends the evening meeting a part-time model(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 19-year-old computer enthusiast goes on the first date of his life, while a music producer and a singer who both enjoyed pop success in the 1970s meet at Fred's restaurant. Other guests in this episode include a PR executive who always finds one little thing that puts her off every man she meets, and two strangers with a love of dancing(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirty-three-year old Lucy comes into St George's having had a severe epileptic seizure that left her unconscious, and consultant Manny reflects on the damage a seizure of such magnitude might have done to her body. Two-year-old Sophie is treated after her brother squirted her in the face with a cleaning product, while Simon arrives in the urgent care centre with severe pain in his neck(n)

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Cameras reveal what can go wrong when a dream holiday turns into a nightmare. Footage includes sunbed dashes, ski crashes, Brits behaving badly and locals behaving madly, all told by the people who were there. Psychologists and industry experts also reflect on the anxieties and situations that many can experience during a break(n)

programme.title: Jamie's Easy Christmas Countdown
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offer his stress-free guide to planning the most important feast of the year, by showing exactly what to cook and when across Christmas Eve and Christmas Day. At the centre of his table is a show-stopping turkey with herb butter and crispy skin. That's surrounded by smoked salmon pate, pork stuffing crammed with silky leeks, sweet peaches and roasted chestnuts, glazed clementine carrots and gnarly sausage and sage(n)

programme.title: Old People's Home for 4 Year Olds
programme.desc: In August, Channel 4 broadcast a two-part documentary which followed an experiment in which 11 pensioners spent six weeks sharing a classroom with young children to see whether it had a positive impact on the OAPs. This festive update finds out whether the elderly volunteers are still showing signs of progress in mood, memory and mobility, and brings the young and old back together for a carol service, at which their families are guests of honour. Rumours go round that Santa may put in a surprise appearance(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton, Matt Tebbutt and Helen Lawal present tips on how to feed family and friend better, cheaper and faster at Christmas. They test three novel turkey-cooking methods against the clock - including deep-frying a whole bird - and share time-saving tips for preparing parsnips. They explore whether champagne is worth its hefty price tag, investigate whether digestif drinks really do help after a big meal and explore the psychology of why people cannot stop dipping into the chocolate box(n)

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: The team investigates a disabled boy's death in an apartment fire, focusing inquiries not only on a tenant who lived in the same building and had been linked to several previous blazes, but also on the victim's parents, who had struggled to cope with the strain of looking after him. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
